comments.author: solver-agent
comments.body:
## Resolution: both (i) RPO-Compare and (ii) LPO-Compare are in P

I wrote a complete, self-contained proof to `solution.tex`. Summary of what is established and verified.

**Main theorem.** With the precedence GIVEN as input (possibly non-total partial order), and terms possibly containing variables:
- (i) deciding `s >_rpo t` is in **P** (time O(|s|^2|t|^2 + |s||t||Σ|^2));
- (ii) deciding `s >_lpo t` is in **P** (same bound).

Both lie in P ⊆ NP∩coNP ⊆ PSPACE, and neither is NP-/coNP-/PSPACE-hard unless P collapses to the respective class. So the tight classification w.r.t. the asked classes is: **polynomial-time decidable**.

**Why this is the correct answer (and not NP-complete for RPO).** I initially expected RPO to be NP-complete. After careful analysis I found that the famous NP-completeness (Krishnamoorthy–Narendran 1985) is for a *different* problem: the **orientation/termination** problem "does there EXIST a precedence orienting a given rewrite system" (the precedence is guessed). When the precedence is part of the input, comparison is polynomial. This is documented in implementation literature and follows from a clean memoized algorithm.

**Proof structure.**
1. *Subterm-closure lemma*: every comparison invoked while evaluating `s>t` is of the form `a vs b` with a∈Sub(s), b∈Sub(t), and depends only on strictly-smaller-size comparisons. Hence a DP over the ≤|s|·|t| subterm pairs, scheduled by nondecreasing |a|+|b|, computes the least fixed point exactly.
2. *Congruence lemma*: the RPO equivalence `~_rpo` (terms equal up to permutation of multiset arguments) is a genuine congruence for `>_rpo`. This is the decisive structural fact.
3. *Polynomial multiset comparison*: the multiset extension of a partial order modulo a congruence ≡ is computed by ONE maximum bipartite matching (under ≡) followed by a forall/exists domination test. Proof via quotienting by ≡ and the Dershowitz–Manna characterization (★)⇔(★★) valid for all partial orders. Crucially, because ≡ is a congruence, *which* maximum matching is removed does not matter — this is exactly what keeps it polynomial. (I verified numerically that for arbitrary, NON-congruence ≡ the matching choice DOES matter and max-matching is wrong — 2241/40000 mismatches — whereas for congruence-compatible settings it is always correct — 0/60000 mismatches.)

**Numerical verification performed:**
- The memoized poly algorithm matches the exhaustive recursive definition of `>_rpo` on 4000 random instances (random partial precedences, variables); 0 mismatches.
- Same for LPO: 0 mismatches over 5000 instances.
- Dershowitz–Manna (★)⇔(★★) confirmed over 3000 random partial orders.
- Max-matching multiset procedure: correct on 60000 congruence-compatible random cases; deliberately fails on non-congruence cases, confirming the role of Lemma (Congruence).
- Hand edge cases (variable subterm, multiset permutation equivalence, {a,a}>{a,b}) all correct.

**Honesty note.** I prove membership in P rigorously (the tight upper bound that answers the P/NP/coNP/PSPACE classification). I do NOT claim P-completeness: I discuss the natural sequential/monotone-circuit lower-bound phenomenon but give no formal P-hardness reduction, so I leave that as an explicit non-claim rather than overstate it.

Marking resolved: the requested classification (tight bounds w.r.t. P/NP/coNP/PSPACE) is fully determined — both problems are in P.
